primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign currency risk arising from intragroup dividend receivables in consolidated financial statements can be designated as a hedged item under hedge accounting rules

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the general requirement that only items with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items, while paragraph 6.3.6 provides an explicit exception permitting intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when FX exposure is not fully eliminated on consolidation.
This Interpretation specifically addresses hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations and clarifies the eligible hedged risks and hedging instruments for such hedges.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs establish the treatment of exchange differences on monetary items forming part of a net investment in a foreign operation, which is the foundational FX exposure at issue.
These paragraphs define eligible hedged items and requirements for hedge accounting, providing the general framework within which the exception for intragroup items operates.

peripheral_authority.reason: Provisions and contingent liabilities are not directly relevant to the hedge accounting treatment of dividend receivables.
Insurance contract measurement requirements are topically unrelated to foreign currency hedge accounting for intragroup dividends.
Distribution of non-cash assets as dividends addresses measurement of dividend payables, not FX hedge accounting for receivables.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for net investment in foreign operation
No hedge accounting - FX recognized in profit or loss
Elimination of intragroup items and FX impact

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for net investment in foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6, the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if it results in an exposure to FX gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation, and IFRIC 16 confirms this applies to hedges of net investments.

approaches.label: Foreign exchange differences recognized in profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If hedge accounting is not elected or does not qualify, IAS 21 paragraph 28 requires exchange differences on monetary items to be recognized in profit or loss in the period they arise.

approaches.label: Intragroup elimination with FX recognition
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 45 establishes that intragroup monetary assets cannot be eliminated against corresponding liabilities without showing the results of currency fluctuations in consolidated financial statements, requiring FX recognition.
